meta.reportLabel: Sun WAR-SHOCK - the de-escalation COLLAPSED: Iran's IRGC hit the GFS Galaxy in Hormuz and declared the Strait CLOSED; US struck ~140 targets Sun (>300 in 3 nights); Iran fired on Gulf states (Jordan/Bahrain/Kuwait/Qatar/UAE/Oman). CENTCOM says the Strait is OPEN - a live shooting contest, not a settled closure. Markets closed Fri (~$71.2 WTI, DXY ~101) - expect GAPS on the open: oil UP, gold UP, risk (AUD/NZD/EUR) DOWN, USD firm. Yen is the two-way (haven bounce vs energy-import hit). Jul 14 US CPI still lands Tue.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The fragile de-escalation collapsed over the weekend into the sharpest escalation since the war began: Iran's IRGC struck the GFS Galaxy in the Strait of Hormuz and declared the Strait CLOSED to all traffic, the US answered with ~140 targets early Sunday (>300 across three nights), and Iran fired missiles and drones at six Gulf states. CENTCOM disputes the closure - the Strait is 'open... Iran does not control the strait' - so this is a live shooting contest, not a settled shutdown. Markets closed Friday before the worst (WTI ~$71.2, DXY ~101, EUR/USD ~1.1415, USD/JPY ~162.5, gold ~$4,140), so expect gaps on the Sunday-evening open: oil sharply higher, gold bid, risk currencies softer, the dollar firm on haven-plus-higher-for-longer. The yen is the pivotal two-way - a war-shock haven bounce vs Japan's total energy-import hit - and Jul 14 US CPI still lands Tuesday.
meta.nextBigEvent: Rolling: Strait of Hormuz - IRGC says CLOSED, CENTCOM says OPEN. A live shooting contest over ~20% of world oil/gas; Oman drafting a traffic-management proposal is the fastest off-ramp. Then Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC US June CPI (headline soft ~3.9%, core firm ~2.9%).

dailyRead: The fragile de-escalation collapsed over the weekend into the worst escalation since the war began - and the board flips from 'less RANGE' to an active risk-off, oil-shock tape. On Jul 11 the IRGC struck the Cyprus-flagged container ship GFS Galaxy in the Strait of Hormuz (one crew missing, fire aboard) and then declared the Strait CLOSED to all traffic 'until further notice.' The US answered with its heaviest strikes in months - ~140 targets early Sunday, >300 across three nights - and Iran fired ballistic missiles and drones at Gulf states (Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, UAE, Oman). CENTCOM says the Strait 'is open... Iran does not control the strait' and US forces are positioned to keep it open, so this is a live shooting contest, not a settled closure. Markets closed Friday before the worst (WTI ~$71.2, DXY ~101, EUR/USD ~1.1415, USD/JPY ~162.5, gold ~$4,140), so expect gaps at the Sunday-evening open: oil sharply higher, gold bid, risk currencies (AUD/NZD, EUR) softer, and the dollar firm on the haven-plus-higher-for-longer combination. The non-obvious wrinkle is the yen: a genuine war-shock could finally hand JPY a haven bounce (and make MoF intervention effective, leaning with the wind), cutting USD/JPY off 162.5 - but Japan importing ~100% of its energy makes a sustained oil shock a terms-of-trade negative that fights the flow. Jul 14 US CPI still lands Tuesday (headline seen soft ~3.9%, core firm ~2.9%), but for the open the geopolitics is the driver. Trade small into a gappy, two-way, headline-run tape; the only clean directional is long oil, and even that is hostage to an Oman-brokered de-escalation.

today.headline: War-shock: gold ~$4,140 set to gap UP on a clean haven bid; oil to spike
today.read: The Strait closure + ~140-target strike wave hand gold a clean safe-haven bid on the open - the haven leg leads even as a firmer USD/steadier yields partly offset. LONG (LOW-MED). A rapid Oman de-escalation deflates the premium; a hot CPI re-arms the real-rate selloff.

today.headline: Petro-currency gets a REAL oil bid - Strait closed, WTI to gap up
today.read: The escalation turns CAD from 'floor' to a genuine terms-of-trade bid that should hold USD/CAD off ~1.42 even vs a haven USD. USD/CAD SHORT tilt (LOW-MED). An Oman de-escalation crushes oil and the CAD bid; the BoC stays sidelined Jul 15.

today.headline: Energy-importer loser - EUR the terms-of-trade hit on the oil shock
today.read: EUR is the classic energy-importer casualty of an oil spike, a direct hit on top of its 1-yr low (~1.1415) vs a haven USD. Short-EUR/USD re-armed and reinforced (MED). A soft US CPI or an oil crash on de-escalation relieves it; a hawkish ECB Jul 23 squeezes shorts.

today.headline: The two-way trap: haven bounce off 162.5 vs Japan's energy-import hit
today.read: A genuine risk-off could finally hand JPY a haven bounce (and let the MoF intervene with the wind), dragging USD/JPY down - but Japan's ~100% energy import bill makes a sustained oil spike yen-NEGATIVE. Trade the two-way, not a direction. BoJ Jul 31 hawkish.

today.headline: Risk-off beta - AUD softens into the war-shock gap
today.read: AUD is a China/risk-beta leg that softens into risk-off, only partly cushioned by its commodity/LNG export status; thin Aug tail (~36%), soft China CPI. RANGE->short tilt. A fast de-escalation + soft US CPI snaps it back up; a hot Q2 CPI re-arms it.

today.headline: Risk-off beta - dovish-hike NZD softens too; gap risk down
today.read: The peaked-3.9%/higher-hurdle floor stands but NZD is high-beta into a war-shock - the gap risk is down. NZ Q2 CPI ~Jul 16 is the domestic swing. Risk-off dominates the CPI; a >4% print re-arms the hawks against the tape.

today.headline: Haven bid ADDS to higher-for-longer - DXY firm ~101 into the gap
today.read: The war-shock stacks a haven bid on top of higher-for-longer, and the oil spike reinforces the no-cut story; DXY ~101. Jul 14 CPI still resets the rate path (headline soft ~3.9%, core firm ~2.9%). A soft headline misread dovish fights the haven bid; a fast de-escalation deflates it.

today.headline: No change - firm but a passenger in a geopolitics-run tape
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, but in a risk-off war tape GBP is an energy-importer passenger that a firm USD caps. UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the domestic check.

strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, SEP 1-2 hikes, zero 2026 cuts. The weekend war-shock (Strait closed, ~140-target strikes) adds a haven bid on top of higher-for-longer; the oil spike reinforces the inflation/no-cut story. DXY ~101 into the gap.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Firm into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; the haven bid + oil-inflation cap the downside, and Jul 14 CPI still resets the rate path - headline seen soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9%) but core firm (+0.3%, 2.9%). Risk: a soft headline misread dovish fights the haven bid; a rapid Oman de-escalation deflates the safe-haven premium. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; a passenger in the geopolitics tape
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). In a war-shock, risk-off tape GBP is an energy-importer passenger that a firm USD caps.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish domestically - services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30 - but risk-off flows overwhelm the rate story near term. Risk: UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the check; a soft UK labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: Strait closed -> petro-currency gets a genuine bid
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5% - but all part-time, mfg -17k); BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold; technical recession. The Strait closure/oil spike is a direct CAD terms-of-trade positive - a real bid, not just a premium.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 The oil spike should hold USD/CAD off its highs even vs a haven USD, but the BoC stays sidelined Jul 15 and the internals are hollow. Risk: an Oman de-escalation crushes oil and the CAD bid with it; a hot US CPI re-fires USD/CAD. Next BoC Jul 15.

strength.tag: 2.50% dovish hike; high-beta, gap risk down
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, but scripted dovish: headline inflation seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. Soft China CPI (+1%). Into a war-shock NZD is a high-beta risk currency - the gap risk is down.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The dovish-hike floor stands but risk-off dominates near term; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: risk-off overwhelms the CPI; a 4%+ print re-arms the hawks against the tape. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: risk/China beta softens; thin Aug tail (~36%)
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. Into a war-shock AUD is a risk/China-beta leg that softens, only partly cushioned by its commodity/LNG export status.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Sticky-core floor vs the risk-off tape - the gap risk is down. Risk: a fast de-escalation snaps it back up; a durable de-escalation + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. EUR is the classic energy-importer loser of an oil shock - a direct terms-of-trade hit on top of the firm-USD tape that pinned it at a 1-yr low (~1.1415).
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Oil-inflation lifts Sept-hike bets but that stays a slow forward story, not a spot bid; the energy shock + firm USD lead near term. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 core tone hands EUR a leg; an oil crash on de-escalation relieves the pressure. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: spot ~162.5; war-shock haven bounce vs energy-import hit
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Spot ~162.5 (near 40-yr lows). The key two-way: a war-shock risk-off could finally give JPY a haven bounce (and let the MoF intervene with the wind), but Japan's ~100% energy import bill makes a sustained oil spike a terms-of-trade negative that fights it. MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Haven flows can drag USD/JPY down fast off 162.5; carry + energy re-grind it up toward 163+ and force the MoF's hand. BoJ Jul 31 hawkish. Risk: whichever of the two legs dominates the gap. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.risk: A sustained oil spike hits JPY (energy importer) harder than AUD and flips it; a rapid de-escalation snaps risk-on; a MoF no-show.

symbols.why: The Strait closure/oil spike is a REAL CAD terms-of-trade bid now (not just a premium) that should hold USD/CAD off ~1.42 even vs a haven USD.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - IRGC 'closed' vs CENTCOM 'open'
catalysts.note: Live shooting contest over the waterway; GFS Galaxy hit, US ~140 targets Sun, Iran strikes on six Gulf states - every headline gaps oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.
Araghchi-Oman met Sat; a drafted Strait-management deal is the fastest de-escalation path - a breakthrough crushes the oil/haven premium.
The 4.2%-regime check - headline seen soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9% on June's lower oil) but core firm (+0.3%, ~2.9%); the July oil spike hits JULY data, not this print.
Hold; oil bid vs hollow internals + technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Hold; core-2.5% + oil-inflation is the Sept-hike signal; the energy-shock complicates the dovish read.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; the oil spike reinforces higher-for-longer.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162.5, the MoF zone hot, and a war-shock in play.
June-17 MoU's negotiating window ends; Hormuz administration the core dispute.

geopolitics: The weekend broke the fragile de-escalation and produced the sharpest escalation since the war began. On Jul 11 Iran's IRGC struck the Cyprus-flagged container ship GFS Galaxy in the Strait of Hormuz - one crew missing, a fire aboard - after firing what it called a 'warning shot' at a vessel using an 'unauthorized route,' and then declared the Strait closed to all traffic 'until further notice.' The US launched its heaviest strikes in months: CENTCOM hit ~140 Iranian targets early Sunday (missile/drone sites, naval capabilities, ammunition depots, coastal surveillance), taking the three-night total above 300 targets. Iran retaliated against Gulf states - Jordan (Prince Hassan Air Base), Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, the UAE and Oman - with ballistic missiles and suicide drones. Crucially, CENTCOM disputes the closure outright: the Strait 'is open to all vessels seeking to lawfully transit... Iran does not control the strait,' and US forces are positioned to ensure freedom of navigation. So the waterway - through which ~20% of the world's oil and gas moves - is a live shooting contest rather than a settled shutdown, with traffic at a standstill. The one de-escalation thread: FM Araghchi met his Omani counterpart Saturday and Oman is drafting a tentative proposal to manage Strait traffic - the fastest off-ramp if it holds. FX transmission (markets closed Friday before the worst - expect gaps on the open): oil should gap sharply higher on a real supply-outage bid; CAD turns from 'floor' to a genuine petro-currency bid that holds USD/CAD off its highs even vs a haven dollar; the USD firms on haven-plus-higher-for-longer (DXY ~101); gold (~$4,140) takes a clean safe-haven bid; EUR is the energy-importer loser; AUD/NZD soften as risk/China-beta legs; GER40 faces a double headwind at record highs; silver is torn between its haven leg and its risk-off industrial leg. The yen is the pivotal two-way - a war-shock haven bounce off 162.5 (with the MoF now able to lean with the wind) vs Japan's ~100% energy-import terms-of-trade hit. The whole complex is a violent two-way: a CENTCOM-secured Strait or an Oman traffic deal can unwind the oil/haven premium overnight, which is why every directional call is sized to a whisper and the board carries no high-conviction pairs into a gappy open with CPI on Tuesday.
